output.code:
# Let's open and read the raw text file
path = "/mnt/data/file-UMivhAGQ61FAoaLd98ho2F-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    raw = f.read()

raw[:1000], len(raw)

# Find all positions of the double quote character in raw text
quote_positions = [i for i, ch in enumerate(raw) if ch == '"']
quote_positions, len(quote_positions)

# Find all occurrences of "قال تعالى:"
subs = []
start = 0
while True:
    idx = raw.find("قال تعالى:", start)
    if idx == -1:
        break
    subs.append(idx)
    start = idx + 1
subs

for idx in subs:
    print(idx, raw[max(0, idx-40): idx+80].replace("\n","\\n"))

# Find all occurrences of the hadith phrase and "حديث:"
needle = "يحرم بالرضاع ما يحرم بالنسب"
positions = []
start = 0
while True:
    idx = raw.find(needle, start)
    if idx == -1:
        break
    positions.append(idx)
    start = idx + 1
positions

import json, re

text = raw

spans = []

# 1) First ayah inside quotes: between quotes positions 165 and 707
# We already know: start_quote_idx=165 end_quote_idx=707
start1 = 165 + 1
end1 = 707
aya1 = text[start1:end1]
aya1[:30], aya1[-30:], len(aya1)

start3 = 1412 + 1
# Find end of line (newline) after start3
newline_idx = text.find("\n", start3)
start3, newline_idx, text[start3:newline_idx]

needle_hadith = "يحرم بالرضاع ما يحرم بالنسب"
pos_hadith_1 = text.find(needle_hadith, 1800, 2000)
pos_hadith_1, text[pos_hadith_1:pos_hadith_1+len(needle_hadith)]

idx_q1 = subs[3]  # 1900
prefix = "قال تعالى:"
assert text[idx_q1:idx_q1+len(prefix)] == prefix
# find first non-space after the colon
i = idx_q1 + len(prefix)
while i < len(text) and text[i] == " ":
    i += 1
start4 = i
# find the index of the first "{" after this
brace_idx = text.find("{", start4)
# set end to the character just before the space preceding "{" if present
# Let's backtrack from brace_idx to skip spaces
j = brace_idx
# back up any spaces
while j > start4 and text[j-1] == " ":
    j -= 1
end4 = j
aya4 = text[start4:end4]
aya4[:40], aya4[-40:], (start4, end4)

idx_q2 = subs[4]  # 3233
# find "(" after idx_q2
paren_open = text.find("(", idx_q2)
paren_close = text.find(")", paren_open+1)
paren_open, paren_close, text[paren_open:paren_close+1][:20]

start6 = paren_open + 1
end6 = paren_close
aya6 = text[start6:end6]
aya6[:60], aya6[-60:], (start6, end6)

spans_calc = [
    ("Aya", 166, 707),
    ("Aya", 1182, 1257),
    ("Aya", 1413, 1483),
    ("Hadeeth", 1871, 1871+len(needle_hadith)),
    ("Aya", 1911, 2454),
    ("Hadeeth", 3203, 3230),
    ("Aya", 3245, 3784),
]

# Validate that text matches per constraints (no leading/trailing whitespace, punctuation?)
def is_clean_span(span_text):
    # no leading/trailing whitespace
    if span_text != span_text.strip():
        return False, "whitespace"
    # no quotes or brackets or punctuation at edges
    bad_chars = set('"\'«»“”()[]{}،.;:!؟…—–- ')
    if span_text and (span_text[0] in bad_chars or span_text[-1] in bad_chars):
        return False, f"edge bad char {span_text[0]} / {span_text[-1]}"
    return True, ""

for typ, s, e in spans_calc:
    st = text[s:e]
    ok, why = is_clean_span(st)
    print(typ, s, e, repr(st[:20]+"..."+st[-20:]), ok, why)
